updates.title: ITR filing 2026: Why waiting until June 15 could help taxpayers avoid notices
updates.summary: The ITR filing deadline for FY 2025-26 is July 31, 2026. Tax experts advise against filing before June 15 to ensure all documents are updated, as early submissions may lead to discrepancies and incorrect reporting in certain cases. Details here.

updates.title: SCSS account rules: Who can invest, how much interest you get and what tax applies
updates.summary: The Senior Citizens Savings Scheme (SCSS) offers Indian retirees a secure investment with guaranteed returns. Eligible individuals can invest a minimum of  ₹1,000, with a maximum limit of  ₹30 lakh. The scheme provides attractive quarterly interest payouts and tax benefits under Section 80C.
updates.user_impact: May affect your tax liability or filing process
updates.content_angle: Educational explainer on this taxation development

updates.title: How to divide monthly salary between rent, EMI, SIP, insurance and savings?
updates.summary: Effective management of monthly salary is crucial for financial stability. The 50/30/20 rule allocates income into essential expenses (50%), lifestyle spending (30%), and savings (20%), promoting disciplined budgeting, investment, and insurance coverage for long-term wealth building.
